title: Purchase Discounts — Gross vs Net Method
event: Hollis Co. buys $10,000 of inventory on terms 2/10, n/30 and pays AFTER the discount window closes. Prepare the purchase and payment entries.

lines.label: invoice at face
lines.why: Gross method records the full invoice and reacts only if the discount is taken.
lines.trap: Simple, but it HIDES the cost of missed discounts inside inventory.

lines.label: full invoice
lines.trap: Nothing about the terms is recorded yet under gross.

entries.label: Pay late — no news, says gross

lines.label: settled
lines.trap: The missed 200 vanishes silently into inventory cost — that's the gross method's blindness.

lines.label: full price
lines.trap: No expense, no signal — management never sees the inefficiency.

lines.label: invoice net of discount
lines.why: Net method assumes discounts WILL be taken — the true cash price is the real cost.
lines.trap: Theoretically superior: inventory carries the price an efficient payer would pay.

lines.label: net obligation
lines.trap: If paid within the window, payment is a clean 9,800 — no adjustment.

entries.label: Pay late — the miss gets a name

lines.label: net obligation cleared
lines.trap: The extra 200 paid is NOT more inventory cost — the goods didn't get more valuable.

lines.label: financing penalty exposed
lines.why: The missed discount surfaces as a financing expense management can see and fix.
lines.trap: An expense/other-item line — the net method's whole advantage is making this visible.

lines.label: full invoice
lines.why: Full price paid after the window.
lines.trap: 2% for 20 days ≈ 36% annualized — Purchase Discounts Lost is an expensive line to grow.

memorize.body: Gross buries missed discounts in inventory; net exposes them as Purchase Discounts Lost. Same cash, different visibility.
Under net, paying LATE is the event that triggers an extra line — paying on time is the quiet case. Gross is the reverse.

questions.prompt: Net method, $10,000 invoice at 2/10 n/30, paid on day 25. Purchase Discounts Lost = ?

distractors.feedback: Day 25 is past the window. Under net, the missed 2% must surface as Purchase Discounts Lost: 200.
That's the recorded payable. The LOST DISCOUNT is just the 2% penalty: 200.

build.scaffold: Gross records face and stays silent; net records the cash price and names the penalty when you're late.
